description: Figure 9 presents planar projections of Young's modulus for the Fe₁₋ₓMnₓAl alloy system (x ranging from 0 to 1) on (001) and (110) crystallographic planes, illustrating the directional dependence and elastic anisotropy calculated from first-principles DFT. The left subplot displays the (001) plane projection with axes oriented along [100] and [010] directions, while the right subplot shows the (110) plane projection along [1̄10] and [001] directions, with Young's modulus values plotted in GPa. Nine distinct compositions are represented by different line styles: FeAl, Fe₀.₈₇₅Mn₀.₁₂₅Al, Fe₀.₇₅Mn₀.₂₅Al, Fe₀.₆₂₅Mn₀.₃₇₅Al, Fe₀.₅Mn₀.₅Al, Fe₀.₃₇₅Mn₀.₆₂₅Al, Fe₀.₂₅Mn₀.₇₅Al, Fe₀.₁₂₅Mn₀.₈₇₅Al, and MnAl. The distinctly non-spherical, butterfly-shaped contour patterns demonstrate strong elastic anisotropy across all compositions. As reported in the caption and citation text, Fe₀.₁₂₅Mn₀.₈₇₅Al exhibits the maximum Young's modulus along the principal crystallographic directions, while MnAl shows the minimum values. These polar plots visually confirm that the planar contours deviate significantly from circular shapes, supporting the conclusion that Fe-Mn-Al alloys possess substantial directional variation in stiffness essential for high-temperature structural applications.
